channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea’s parliament passes landmark ban on production and sales of dog meat
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Tuesday passed a landmark ban on production and sales of dog meat, as public calls for a prohibition have grown sharply over concerns about animal rights and the country’s international image.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Live updates | Blinken seeks to contain the war as fighting rages in Gaza and Israel strikes Lebanon
item.description: On an urgent mission aimed primarily at preventing the Israel-Hamas conflict from spreading into a regional war, U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Airlines say they found loose parts in door panels during inspections of Boeing Max 9 jets
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Federal investigators say a door panel slid up before flying off an Alaska Airlines jetliner last week, and they are looking at whether four bolts that were supposed to help hold the panel in place might have been missing when the plane took off.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, CLAIRE RUSH and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New focus in military spending can keep Pentagon step ahead of China: expert
item.description: The Fiscal Year 2024 National Defense Authorization Act has a new focus on military development of artificial intelligence while also putting guardrails on the new tech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some are leaving earthquake-rattled Wajima. But this Japanese fish seller is determined to rebuild
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Yoshie Minamidani’s heart leapt when she saw the stray tabby cat, a mainstay of the famous Asaichi Dori shopping street in Wajima city on the western coast of Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Argentines ask folk cowboy saint Gauchito Gil to help cope with galloping inflation
item.description: MERCEDES, Argentina (AP) — Watching his savings being eaten away by Argentina’s rampant inflation, Jorge Zabala turned to what some might consider an unexpected place for financial help: Argentina’s folk cowboy saint Gauchito Gil.
item.dc:creator: NATACHA PISARENKO and ALMUDENA CALATRAVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Central US walloped by blizzard conditions, closing highways, schools and government offices
item.description: MADISON, Wis, (AP) — A sprawling storm that pelted much of the nation's midsection with more than a half a foot of snow and gusty winds created whiteout conditions that closed parts of two interstate highways and prompted officials to close schools and government offices in several states Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: AP, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to return to federal court as judges hear arguments on whether he is immune from prosecution
item.description: Donald Trump is set to return for the first time in months to the federal courthouse in Washington as an appeals court hears arguments on whether the former president is immune from prosecution on charges that he plotted to overturn the results of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Alanna Durkin Richer And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he hopes economy crashes in next 12 months: ‘I don’t want to be Herbert Hoover’
item.description: Former President Trump said in an interview that aired Monday that he predicts the U.S. economy will crash and that he hopes it does so within the next year.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At trial, a Russian billionaire blames Sotheby's for losing millions on art by Picasso, da Vinci
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sotheby’s defended itself at a trial Monday against accusations that it helped defraud a Russian oligarch out of tens of millions of dollars, saying it knew nothing of wrongdoing by an art buyer who advised the billionaire on buying works by famed artists like Amedeo Modigliani and L
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video of 73-year-old boarded up inside his apartment sparks investigation
item.description: HARVEY, Ill. (AP) — Rudolph Williams says he was home in a Chicago suburb when he realized the doors and windows to his courtyard-style apartment had been boarded up with plywood, locking him inside.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man told police he drank wine, cough syrup before Henderson crash that killed mother, 2 sons: police
item.description: The man accused of causing a crash that killed a mother and two of her children told responders that he had wine and cough syrup prior to the crash, according to an arrest report.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: US has ‘done very little’ responding to attacks on Americans in Middle East: Sen. Joni Ernst
item.description: Ernst criticized the administration for not conveying a decisive stance and stressed the importance of demonstrating strength on the global stage.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Florida father helped cover up son’s crime scene after 18-month-old child shot, killed: sheriff
item.description: The Flagler County Sheriff's Office arrested a man for allegedly helping to cover up a crime scene after his son was charged in the deadly shooting of an 18-month-old child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charges filed in death of Texas teen who suffered severe burn injuries
item.description: A 23-year-old man is facing manslaughter charges after a North Texas teen girl died from severe burn injuries she received last month, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Students rally at the Iowa Capitol days after Perry school shooting
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — This year's Iowa legislative session kicked off Monday with student protestors filling the Capitol rotunda to call for stricter gun laws just days after a school shooting in Perry, Iowa, that left one 11-year-old boy dead and seven other people wounded.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Park Service retracts decision to take down William Penn statue at Philadelphia historical site
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The National Park Service withdrew a proposal Monday to take down a statue of William Penn at a Philadelphia historical site as part of a renovation that touched off a torrent of criticism over the legacy of the man who founded the province of Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US rents down 3.2% from summer peak
item.description: If you're thinking about negotiating your lease in 2024, now is the time, according to ApartmentList analysts.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: How to tell if you're flying on a Boeing 737 MAX 9
item.description: The pressure of the ejected “plug,” which was used to fill an optional emergency exit door on the plane, was enough to rip the shirt off a person seated near the missing panel, one witness told Nexstar's KOIN 6 News.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: 'King of the NRA': Civil trial scrutinizes lavish spending by gun rights group’s longtime leader
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The longtime head of National Rifle Association operated as the “King of the NRA,” spending lavishly on himself, punishing dissent and showering allies with country club memberships and no-show contracts, a lawyer for the New York attorney general’s office told jurors Monday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 911 transcripts reveal chaotic scene as gunman killed 18 people in Maine
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Callers who dialed 911 after Maine's deadliest mass shooting described hiding behind trees, scrambling to find their glasses, searching for loved ones and fearing for their lives as shots rang out first at a bowling alley, then minutes later at a bar.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Memphis judge maintains $1 million bond for man charged with firing shots at Jewish school
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A judge on Monday declined to reduce a $1 million bond for a man charged with shooting at a contractor at a Jewish school in Memphis, after his attorney requested a lower amount to allow him to secure his release and get proper mental health treatment outside of jail.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lisa Bonet files for divorce from Jason Momoa 18 years after they became a couple
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Lisa Bonet has filed for divorce from Jason Momoa 18 years after the two actors became a couple. The 56-year-old Bonet, whose legal name is Lilakoi Moon, filed documents to end her marriage to the 44-year-old Momoa in Los Angeles County court on Monday.
item.dc:creator: AP, KRON4

item.title: US Supreme Court declines to hear 2nd Illinois case challenging state's ban on semiautomatic weapons
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear a second Illinois case challenging the state's ban on semiautomatic weapons, which took effect Jan. 1. Decatur Republican state Rep.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Moon landing attempt by U.S. company may be doomed
item.description: The first U.S. moon landing attempt in more than 50 years appeared to be doomed after a private company's spacecraft developed a “critical” fuel leak just hours after Monday's launch.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: First Lady Jill Biden, Halle Berry to visit University of Illinois Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — First Lady Jill Biden is set to make an appearance in Chicago on Thursday. According to the White House, the First Lady is scheduled to visit the University of Illinois Chicago alongside actress and women’s health advocate, Halle Berry.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at historic Texas hotel injures 21 and scatters debris in downtown Fort Worth
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — An explosion at a historic Texas hotel in Fort Worth on Monday blew out windows, littered downtown streets with large sections of debris from the building and injured 21 people, including one person who was in critical condition, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada judge attacked by defendant in viral court video finishes sentencing him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man who leaped over a judicial bench and attacked a Nevada judge on video last week was shackled and closely watched Monday as he appeared before the same judge and was sentenced to up to four years in state prison.
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 'Multiple' people hurt after explosion at Fort Worth hotel, authorities say
item.description: Aerial footage in Fort Worth, Texas, showed debris from a hotel scattered across downtown streets Monday as authorities said they were responding to a “major incident” and urged people to avoid the area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Which US airlines use the Boeing 737 MAX 9 planes?
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- The Federal Aviation Administration issued an order on Friday grounding all Boeing 737 MAX 9 aircraft after an incident on Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 that resulted in a part of the plane blowing out mid-flight.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dry January: What an alcohol-free month can do for your health
item.description: Of those who participated in Dry January in 2018, 71% had improved sleep, 58% lost weight, 54% saw an improvement in their skin and 67% had more energy, according to a study.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, The Hill

item.title: Alaska Air 1282: Man finds passenger's iPhone, says it's 'perfectly' intact after falling from sky
item.description: Law enforcement officials in Washington County are urging anyone who finds potential debris from Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 to reach out to authorities after the NTSB disclosed late Saturday the blown out part of the aircraft could be around Barnes Road near Hwy 217 and the Cedar Hills neighborhood
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: World is more hopeful for 2024: Report
item.description: Public sentiment on AI is divided with 56% expecting positive outcomes such as AI-assisted medical practices while 64% globally anticipate job losses.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, The Hill

item.title: Bottle washes up in France 3 years after South Carolina fisherman dropped it in sea
item.description: A South Carolina fisherman who was asked to drop a memorial bottle 50 miles off the coast in 2021 says it was recently found in France.
item.dc:creator: Adrianna Lawrence, Fox 5

item.title: Headless body found in California vineyard 13 years ago identified, case still unsolved
item.description: In a stunning revelation, a decapitated body found nearly 13 years ago in a vineyard outside Arvin has been identified.
item.dc:creator: Jason Kotowski, Fox 5

item.title: In first interview since conviction, Jonathan Majors says he hopes to work in Hollywood again
item.description: In his first interview since his assault and harassment conviction last month, actor Jonathan Majors said he hopes to work in Hollywood again. “I pray I do, but it’s God’s plan and God’s timing,” Majors said in the interview that aired Monday on ABC’s “Good Morning America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: United Airlines finds loose bolts, other issues on a key part of grounded Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners
item.description: The decision by Alaska Airlines to stop flying one of its planes over the Pacific Ocean to Hawaii due to warnings from a cabin-pressurization system — yet keep flying it over land — is raising questions about whether the jet should have been in the air at all.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush And David Koenig, Associated Press, KPRC 2
